title: Why do AI company logos look like buttholes?
excerpt: A humorous exploration of the uncanny resemblance between AI company logos and human anatomy. Discover why circular, gradient-based designs dominate the AI industry, and what this design convergence tells us about branding in tech.
content_html:
If you pay attention to AI company branding, you'll notice a pattern:

  1. Circular shape (often with a gradient)
  2. Central opening or focal point
  3. Radiating elements from the center
  4. Soft, organic curves

Sound familiar? It should, because it's also an apt description of... well, you know.

A butthole.

The circular AI logo epidemic

If you ever thought that AI company logos look like buttholes, you're not alone.

FastCompany noticed this trend in 2023 and published an article about it, but (I could only presume) their editors and lawyers didn't let them title the article the way the wanted it to title, so it got published with a more safe for work title: The AI boom is creating a new logo trend: the swirling hexagon. They also were careful not to mention anything anatomical.

I don't have editors or lawyers, so let's take a closer look at some examples:

OpenAI's logo evolution

OpenAI's original logo was a simple, text-based mark. Then came the redesign: a perfect circle with a subtle gradient and central void.

OpenAI's official explanation is a masterclass in corporate euphemism:

"The Blossom logo is more than just a visual symbol; it represents the core philosophy that guides our approach to design and innovation. At its heart, the logo captures the dynamic intersection between humanity and technology—two forces that shape our world and inspire our work. The design embodies the fluidity and warmth of human-centered thinking through the use of circles, while right angles introduce the precision and structure that technology demands."

Sure, Sam.

Translation: "We made a circular shape with some angles because it looked nice, then wrote flowery language to justify why our butthole-adjacent design is actually profound."

The fluidity and warmth of human-centered thinking through the use of circles is perhaps the most elegant way anyone has ever described making a logo that resembles an anus.

The Big AI companies

Looking at the logos of the Big AI companies, you can see that they almost all of them have a circular or snowflake-like shape and a central opening.

Only DeepSeek and Midjourney don't follow the trend. Interestingly, both are sea-related.

Smoking gun: Anthropic's Claude

Up until this point, the logos have been subtle. You might say that the logos are simply circular and there's not much more to it.

But Anthropic's Claude takes it to the next level.

Here's a side-by-side comparison with a drawing from Kurt Vonnegut's book "Breakfast of Champions". I added Claude's logo below for easy comparison.

Both the drawing and the description in the book are unambiguous. This is not just "a circular shape with a gradient" anymore, is it?

July 2026 update: the smoking gun now moves

Since publishing this article, I've discovered new evidence. Open claude.ai and click on the Claude logo. Just watch what it does:

The Claude logo, when clicked. I have no further questions.

Every click makes the logo clench and relax. It even responds with a slightly annoyed "Yes, yes. What can I do for you?", as if you poked something you weren't supposed to.

At this point, there's no argument you could make that would persuade me this is not a butthole 🙂

It's not just AI companies

Even traditional companies aren't immune. Here are a few notable or funny examples. But the percentage of AI company logos that look like buttholes is still significantly higher than than any other industry.

I especially like the Electrolux one. It's simple, memorable, and once you see a butt and bikini, you can't unsee it.

Why does this keep happening?

There are several factors at play:

Circular design psychology

Circles represent wholeness, completion, and infinity—concepts that align with AI's promise. They're also friendly and non-threatening, qualities companies desperately want to project when selling potentially job-replacing technology.

Unintentional biomimicry

The human brain finds familiar patterns in random shapes (pareidolia), like a face on Mars, taken by the Viking 1 orbiter and released by NASA in 1976.

But sometimes, designers inadvertently recreate biological forms without realizing the... anatomical implications.

The copycat effect

Once a few major players adopted the circular sphincter aesthetic, everyone followed suit. Now we have an industry where standing out means looking exactly like everyone else's butthole.

Basically, the same reason why so many brands change their logos and look like everyone else.

Design by committee

Another factor is how these logos are created. Important corporate decisions involve many stakeholders. The result is often the safest, most inoffensive option, the average of everyone's opinions. In design meetings at AI companies, conversations probably sound like:

  • Can we make it more futuristic?
  • It needs to feel advanced but approachable.
  • Let's add a subtle gradient to convey intelligence.

No single person suggests making a logo that resembles an anus, but when everyone's feedback gets incorporated, that's what often emerges. Risk aversion in corporate environments naturally pushes designs toward familiar, "safe" territory, which apparently means anatomical openings.

What this says about tech branding

This phenomenon reveals something deeper about the tech industry: the fear of standing out too much. Despite claims of innovation and disruption, there's tremendous pressure to look legitimate by conforming to established visual language.

When OpenAI's sphincter-like logo became successful, it created a template that said, "This is what serious AI looks like." Now, any new AI company that doesn't resemble a colorful anatomical opening risks being seen as unserious or unprofessional.

Tech design trends through history

This isn't the first time tech design has gone through a conformity phase. Consider these previous waves:

  • 1990s-2000s: 3D and Glossy - Remember when every logo needed a drop shadow and a glassy shine? Apple's aqua interface set the standard.
  • 2010-2013: Skeuomorphism - Digital designs mimicking physical objects, with stitched leather textures and realistic dials.
  • 2013-2018: Flat Design - Reaction to skeuomorphism brought minimal, clean interfaces with bright colors and no shadows.
  • 2018-2022: Neomorphism - Soft shadows and semi-flat design creating subtle, "touchable" interfaces.
  • 2022-Present: The Butthole Era - Circular gradients with central focal points dominating AI branding.

Each era started with innovations that were quickly copied until the industry reached saturation point and moved on to the next trend.

Logos become increasingly interchangeable (one of the bags is real, but they all look the same)

Historic logo disasters: You're not alone

AI companies can take some comfort in knowing they're not the first to face unintended anatomical comparisons. Logo history is filled with disasters but to keep this consistent with the theme of the article, here's a couple of them.

  • Zune logo, when flipped, says something different. Maybe that's one of the reasons why iPod won?
  • Brazilian Institute of Oriental Studies is a stylized pagoda silhouetted against the setting sun. Or so the designers wanted it to look. The final result was much more... anatomical. They since changed it to something less suggestive.

Maybe companies should have a panel of "middle schoolers" on their payroll to review logos before launch. They'll find every possible inappropriate interpretation with ruthless efficiency.

Breaking free from the butthole

For companies brave enough to differentiate, here are some alternatives:

  1. Embrace sharp angles - geometric shapes with defined edges create a distinct visual identity
  2. Use negative space creatively - think FedEx arrow, not biological openings
  3. Avoid radial symmetry - not everything needs to be perfectly circular
  4. Skip the gradient - flat design still works
  5. Test with diverse audiences - if five different people independently say "that looks like a butthole," it probably does (show it to teenagers if you want to uncover even the most subtle anatomical implications)

Conclusion

Does this mean AI companies should change their branding? Not necessarily. The familiarity clearly works in building trust. But perhaps the next wave of AI innovation could be accompanied by some visual innovation too.

For companies looking to break the mold, consider these approaches that successful tech brands have used:

  • Embrace meaningful abstraction - Slack's hashtag-inspired logo communicates collaboration without circular clichés
  • Leverage letterforms - Netflix's simple "N" has become instantly recognizable without anatomical confusion
  • Tell a story - Stripe's distinctive parallel lines reflect payment flows moving seamlessly
  • Use distinctive color combinations - Twitch's purple branding stands out in a sea of blue tech logos

The challenge for the next generation of AI companies isn't just technological - it's finding visual language that communicates innovation without resorting to the same tired sphincter-inspired patterns.

PS. This post is meant to be humorous, but let's not pretend there isn't a serious point here about the depressing sameness in modern design. No actual anuses were consulted during this research, though several designers were clearly thinking about them.

If you like what you see, you'll find more stuff like this on my Twitter.

sentences.en: What this says about tech branding
sentences.zh: 这对科技品牌有何说明

blocks.html_en: This phenomenon reveals something deeper about the tech industry: the fear of standing out too much. Despite claims of innovation and disruption, there's tremendous pressure to look legitimate by conforming to established visual language.

sentences.en: This phenomenon reveals something deeper about the tech industry: the fear of standing out too much.
Despite claims of innovation and disruption, there's tremendous pressure to look legitimate by conforming to established visual language.

blocks.html_en: When OpenAI's sphincter-like logo became successful, it created a template that said, "This is what serious AI looks like." Now, any new AI company that doesn't resemble a colorful anatomical opening risks being seen as unserious or unprofessional.

sentences.en: When OpenAI's sphincter-like logo became successful, it created a template that said, "This is what serious AI looks like."
Now, any new AI company that doesn't resemble a colorful anatomical opening risks being seen as unserious or unprofessional.

blocks.html_en: Tech design trends through history

sentences.en: Tech design trends through history
sentences.zh: 历史上的技术设计趋势

blocks.html_en: This isn't the first time tech design has gone through a conformity phase. Consider these previous waves:

sentences.en: This isn't the first time tech design has gone through a conformity phase.
Consider these previous waves:

blocks.html_en: • 1990s-2000s: 3D and Glossy - Remember when every logo needed a drop shadow and a glassy shine? Apple's aqua interface set the standard.

sentences.en: 1990s-2000s: 3D and Glossy - Remember when every logo needed a drop shadow and a glassy shine?
Apple's aqua interface set the standard.

blocks.html_en: • 2010-2013: Skeuomorphism - Digital designs mimicking physical objects, with stitched leather textures and realistic dials.

sentences.en: 2010-2013: Skeuomorphism - Digital designs mimicking physical objects, with stitched leather textures and realistic dials.
sentences.zh: 2010-2013：拟物化——模仿物理物体的数字设计，具有缝合的皮革纹理和逼真的表盘。

blocks.html_en: • 2013-2018: Flat Design - Reaction to skeuomorphism brought minimal, clean interfaces with bright colors and no shadows.

sentences.en: 2013-2018: Flat Design - Reaction to skeuomorphism brought minimal, clean interfaces with bright colors and no shadows.
sentences.zh: 2013-2018：扁平化设计——对拟物化的反应带来了最小、干净的界面，色彩明亮，没有阴影。

blocks.html_en: • 2018-2022: Neomorphism - Soft shadows and semi-flat design creating subtle, "touchable" interfaces.

sentences.en: 2018-2022: Neomorphism - Soft shadows and semi-flat design creating subtle, "touchable" interfaces.
sentences.zh: 2018-2022：新形态 - 柔和的阴影和半平面设计创造微妙的“可触摸”界面。

blocks.html_en: • 2022-Present: The Butthole Era - Circular gradients with central focal points dominating AI branding.

sentences.en: 2022-Present: The Butthole Era - Circular gradients with central focal points dominating AI branding.
sentences.zh: 2022 年至今：屁眼时代 - 具有中心焦点的圆形渐变主导人工智能品牌。

blocks.html_en: Each era started with innovations that were quickly copied until the industry reached saturation point and moved on to the next trend.

sentences.en: Each era started with innovations that were quickly copied until the industry reached saturation point and moved on to the next trend.
sentences.zh: 每个时代都以创新开始，这些创新很快被复制，直到行业达到饱和点并转向下一个趋势。

blocks.html_en: Logos become increasingly interchangeable (one of the bags is real, but they all look the same)

sentences.en: Logos become increasingly interchangeable (one of the bags is real, but they all look the same)
sentences.zh: 标志变得越来越可互换（其中一个包是真的，但它们看起来都一样）

blocks.html_en: Historic logo disasters: You're not alone

sentences.en: Historic logo disasters: You're not alone
sentences.zh: 历史性的标志灾难：你并不孤单

blocks.html_en: AI companies can take some comfort in knowing they're not the first to face unintended anatomical comparisons. Logo history is filled with disasters but to keep this consistent with the theme of the article, here's a couple of them.

sentences.en: AI companies can take some comfort in knowing they're not the first to face unintended anatomical comparisons.
Logo history is filled with disasters but to keep this consistent with the theme of the article, here's a couple of them.

blocks.html_en: • Zune logo, when flipped, says something different. Maybe that's one of the reasons why iPod won?

sentences.en: Zune logo, when flipped, says something different.
Maybe that's one of the reasons why iPod won?

blocks.html_en: • Brazilian Institute of Oriental Studies is a stylized pagoda silhouetted against the setting sun. Or so the designers wanted it to look. The final result was much more... anatomical. They since changed it to something less suggestive.

sentences.en: Brazilian Institute of Oriental Studies is a stylized pagoda silhouetted against the setting sun.
Or so the designers wanted it to look.
The final result was much more...
anatomical.
They since changed it to something less suggestive.

blocks.html_en: Maybe companies should have a panel of "middle schoolers" on their payroll to review logos before launch. They'll find every possible inappropriate interpretation with ruthless efficiency.

sentences.en: Maybe companies should have a panel of "middle schoolers" on their payroll to review logos before launch.
They'll find every possible inappropriate interpretation with ruthless efficiency.

blocks.html_en: Breaking free from the butthole

sentences.en: Breaking free from the butthole
sentences.zh: 从屁眼里挣脱出来

blocks.html_en: For companies brave enough to differentiate, here are some alternatives:

sentences.en: For companies brave enough to differentiate, here are some alternatives:
sentences.zh: 对于勇于差异化的公司来说，以下是一些替代方案：

blocks.html_en: • Embrace sharp angles - geometric shapes with defined edges create a distinct visual identity

sentences.en: Embrace sharp angles - geometric shapes with defined edges create a distinct visual identity
sentences.zh: 拥抱锐角——具有明确边缘的几何形状创造出独特的视觉形象

blocks.html_en: • Use negative space creatively - think FedEx arrow, not biological openings

sentences.en: Use negative space creatively - think FedEx arrow, not biological openings
sentences.zh: 创造性地利用负空间——想想联邦快递的箭头，而不是生物开口

blocks.html_en: • Avoid radial symmetry - not everything needs to be perfectly circular

sentences.en: Avoid radial symmetry - not everything needs to be perfectly circular
sentences.zh: 避免径向对称 - 并非所有东西都需要是完美的圆形

blocks.html_en: • Skip the gradient - flat design still works

sentences.en: Skip the gradient - flat design still works
sentences.zh: 跳过渐变——扁平化设计仍然有效

blocks.html_en: • Test with diverse audiences - if five different people independently say "that looks like a butthole," it probably does (show it to teenagers if you want to uncover even the most subtle anatomical implications)

sentences.en: Test with diverse audiences - if five different people independently say "that looks like a butthole," it probably does (show it to teenagers if you want to uncover even the most subtle anatomical implications)
sentences.zh: 与不同的观众进行测试 - 如果五个不同的人独立地说“这看起来像一个屁眼”，那么它可能就是这样（如果你想揭示最微妙的解剖学含义，请向青少年展示）

blocks.html_en: Conclusion

sentences.en: Conclusion
sentences.zh: 结论

blocks.html_en: Does this mean AI companies should change their branding? Not necessarily. The familiarity clearly works in building trust. But perhaps the next wave of AI innovation could be accompanied by some visual innovation too.

sentences.en: Does this mean AI companies should change their branding?
Not necessarily.
The familiarity clearly works in building trust.
But perhaps the next wave of AI innovation could be accompanied by some visual innovation too.

blocks.html_en: For companies looking to break the mold, consider these approaches that successful tech brands have used:

sentences.en: For companies looking to break the mold, consider these approaches that successful tech brands have used:
sentences.zh: 对于想要打破常规的公司，请考虑成功科技品牌使用的以下方法：

blocks.html_en: • Embrace meaningful abstraction - Slack's hashtag-inspired logo communicates collaboration without circular clichés

sentences.en: Embrace meaningful abstraction - Slack's hashtag-inspired logo communicates collaboration without circular clichés
sentences.zh: 拥抱有意义的抽象——Slack 的主题标签启发徽标传达协作，而不是循环陈词滥调

blocks.html_en: • Leverage letterforms - Netflix's simple "N" has become instantly recognizable without anatomical confusion

sentences.en: Leverage letterforms - Netflix's simple "N" has become instantly recognizable without anatomical confusion
sentences.zh: 利用字母形式 - Netflix 的简单“N”已变得易于识别，不会造成解剖学混乱

blocks.html_en: • Tell a story - Stripe's distinctive parallel lines reflect payment flows moving seamlessly

sentences.en: Tell a story - Stripe's distinctive parallel lines reflect payment flows moving seamlessly
sentences.zh: 讲一个故事 - Stripe 独特的平行线反映了无缝移动的支付流程

blocks.html_en: • Use distinctive color combinations - Twitch's purple branding stands out in a sea of blue tech logos

sentences.en: Use distinctive color combinations - Twitch's purple branding stands out in a sea of blue tech logos
sentences.zh: 使用独特的颜色组合 - Twitch 的紫色品牌在蓝色科技徽标的海洋中脱颖而出

blocks.html_en: The challenge for the next generation of AI companies isn't just technological - it's finding visual language that communicates innovation without resorting to the same tired sphincter-inspired patterns.

sentences.en: The challenge for the next generation of AI companies isn't just technological - it's finding visual language that communicates innovation without resorting to the same tired sphincter-inspired patterns.
sentences.zh: 下一代人工智能公司面临的挑战不仅仅是技术方面的，而是找到能够传达创新的视觉语言，而不是诉诸同样令人厌倦的受括约肌启发的模式。

blocks.html_en: PS. This post is meant to be humorous, but let's not pretend there isn't a serious point here about the depressing sameness in modern design. No actual anuses were consulted during this research, though several designers were clearly thinking about them.

sentences.en: PS.
This post is meant to be humorous, but let's not pretend there isn't a serious point here about the depressing sameness in modern design.
No actual anuses were consulted during this research, though several designers were clearly thinking about them.
If you like what you see, you'll find more stuff like this on my Twitter.
sentences.zh: 如果您喜欢所看到的内容，您会在我的 Twitter 上找到更多类似的内容。

sentences.en: If you like what you see, you'll find more stuff like this on my Twitter.
sentences.zh: 如果您喜欢所看到的内容，您会在我的 Twitter 上找到更多类似的内容。

content_html_zh:
如果你关注人工智能公司的品牌，你会注意到一个模式：

  1. 圆形（通常带有渐变）
  2. 中央开口或焦点
  3. 从中心辐射元件
  4. 柔和、有机的曲线

Sound familiar? It should, because it's also an apt description of... well, you know.

一个屁眼。

圆形AI标志流行

如果你曾经认为人工智能公司的标志看起来像屁眼，那么你并不孤单。

FastCompany noticed this trend in 2023 and published an article about it, but (I could only presume) their editors and lawyers didn't let them title the article the way the wanted it to title, so it got published with a more safe for work title: The AI boom is creating a new logo trend: the swirling hexagon. They also were careful not to mention anything anatomical.

我没有编辑或律师，所以让我们仔细看看一些例子：

OpenAI 的标志演变

OpenAI's original logo was a simple, text-based mark. Then came the redesign: a perfect circle with a subtle gradient and central void.

OpenAI官方的解释堪称企业委婉说法的大师班：

"The Blossom logo is more than just a visual symbol; it represents the core philosophy that guides our approach to design and innovation. At its heart, the logo captures the dynamic intersection between humanity and technology—two forces that shape our world and inspire our work. The design embodies the fluidity and warmth of human-centered thinking through the use of circles, while right angles introduce the precision and structure that technology demands."

当然，萨姆。

翻译：“我们做了一个带有一些角度的圆形形状，因为它看起来不错，然后写了华丽的语言来证明为什么我们的屁眼相邻设计实际上是深刻的。”

通过使用圆圈来实现以人为本的思维的流动性和温暖性，也许是人们所描述的制作类似肛门的标志的最优雅的方式。

大型人工智能公司

看看大人工智能公司的标志，你会发现它们几乎都是圆形或雪花状的形状，中央有一个开口。

Only DeepSeek and Midjourney don't follow the trend. Interestingly, both are sea-related.

确凿证据：Anthropic 的克劳德

Up until this point, the logos have been subtle. You might say that the logos are simply circular and there's not much more to it.

但 Anthropic 的克劳德将其提升到了一个新的水平。

Here's a side-by-side comparison with a drawing from Kurt Vonnegut's book "Breakfast of Champions". I added Claude's logo below for easy comparison.

Both the drawing and the description in the book are unambiguous. This is not just "a circular shape with a gradient" anymore, is it?

2026 年 7 月更新：铁证如山

Since publishing this article, I've discovered new evidence. Open claude.ai and click on the Claude logo. Just watch what it does:

The Claude logo, when clicked. I have no further questions.

Every click makes the logo clench and relax. It even responds with a slightly annoyed "Yes, yes. What can I do for you?", as if you poked something you weren't supposed to.

在这一点上，你无法提出任何论据来说服我这不是一个混蛋🙂

不仅仅是人工智能公司

Even traditional companies aren't immune. Here are a few notable or funny examples. But the percentage of AI company logos that look like buttholes is still significantly higher than than any other industry.

I especially like the Electrolux one. It's simple, memorable, and once you see a butt and bikini, you can't unsee it.

为什么这种情况不断发生？

有几个因素在起作用：

循环设计心理学

Circles represent wholeness, completion, and infinity—concepts that align with AI's promise. They're also friendly and non-threatening, qualities companies desperately want to project when selling potentially job-replacing technology.

无意识的仿生学

人类大脑会在随机形状中发现熟悉的模式（空想性错觉），例如由 Viking 1 轨道飞行器拍摄并于 1976 年由 NASA 发布的火星上的一张脸。

But sometimes, designers inadvertently recreate biological forms without realizing the... anatomical implications.

模仿效应

Once a few major players adopted the circular sphincter aesthetic, everyone followed suit. Now we have an industry where standing out means looking exactly like everyone else's butthole.

基本上，这也是许多品牌改变标志、看起来和其他品牌一样的原因。

委员会设计

Another factor is how these logos are created. Important corporate decisions involve many stakeholders. The result is often the safest, most inoffensive option, the average of everyone's opinions. In design meetings at AI companies, conversations probably sound like:

  • 我们可以让它变得更有未来感吗？
  • 它需要给人一种先进但平易近人的感觉。
  • 让我们添加一个微妙的渐变来传达情报。

No single person suggests making a logo that resembles an anus, but when everyone's feedback gets incorporated, that's what often emerges. Risk aversion in corporate environments naturally pushes designs toward familiar, "safe" territory, which apparently means anatomical openings.

这对科技品牌有何说明

This phenomenon reveals something deeper about the tech industry: the fear of standing out too much. Despite claims of innovation and disruption, there's tremendous pressure to look legitimate by conforming to established visual language.

When OpenAI's sphincter-like logo became successful, it created a template that said, "This is what serious AI looks like." Now, any new AI company that doesn't resemble a colorful anatomical opening risks being seen as unserious or unprofessional.

历史上的技术设计趋势

This isn't the first time tech design has gone through a conformity phase. Consider these previous waves:

  • 1990s-2000s: 3D and Glossy - Remember when every logo needed a drop shadow and a glassy shine? Apple's aqua interface set the standard.
  • 2010-2013：拟物化——模仿物理物体的数字设计，具有缝合的皮革纹理和逼真的表盘。
  • 2013-2018：扁平化设计——对拟物化的反应带来了最小、干净的界面，色彩明亮，没有阴影。
  • 2018-2022：新形态 - 柔和的阴影和半平面设计创造微妙的“可触摸”界面。
  • 2022 年至今：屁眼时代 - 具有中心焦点的圆形渐变主导人工智能品牌。

每个时代都以创新开始，这些创新很快被复制，直到行业达到饱和点并转向下一个趋势。

标志变得越来越可互换（其中一个包是真的，但它们看起来都一样）

历史性的标志灾难：你并不孤单

AI companies can take some comfort in knowing they're not the first to face unintended anatomical comparisons. Logo history is filled with disasters but to keep this consistent with the theme of the article, here's a couple of them.

  • Zune logo, when flipped, says something different. Maybe that's one of the reasons why iPod won?
  • Brazilian Institute of Oriental Studies is a stylized pagoda silhouetted against the setting sun. Or so the designers wanted it to look. The final result was much more... anatomical. They since changed it to something less suggestive.

Maybe companies should have a panel of "middle schoolers" on their payroll to review logos before launch. They'll find every possible inappropriate interpretation with ruthless efficiency.

从屁眼里挣脱出来

对于勇于差异化的公司来说，以下是一些替代方案：

  1. 拥抱锐角——具有明确边缘的几何形状创造出独特的视觉形象
  2. 创造性地利用负空间——想想联邦快递的箭头，而不是生物开口
  3. 避免径向对称 - 并非所有东西都需要是完美的圆形
  4. 跳过渐变——扁平化设计仍然有效
  5. 与不同的观众进行测试 - 如果五个不同的人独立地说“这看起来像一个屁眼”，那么它可能就是这样（如果你想揭示最微妙的解剖学含义，请向青少年展示）

结论

Does this mean AI companies should change their branding? Not necessarily. The familiarity clearly works in building trust. But perhaps the next wave of AI innovation could be accompanied by some visual innovation too.

对于想要打破常规的公司，请考虑成功科技品牌使用的以下方法：

  • 拥抱有意义的抽象——Slack 的主题标签启发徽标传达协作，而不是循环陈词滥调
  • 利用字母形式 - Netflix 的简单“N”已变得易于识别，不会造成解剖学混乱
  • 讲一个故事 - Stripe 独特的平行线反映了无缝移动的支付流程
  • 使用独特的颜色组合 - Twitch 的紫色品牌在蓝色科技徽标的海洋中脱颖而出

下一代人工智能公司面临的挑战不仅仅是技术方面的，而是找到能够传达创新的视觉语言，而不是诉诸同样令人厌倦的受括约肌启发的模式。

PS. This post is meant to be humorous, but let's not pretend there isn't a serious point here about the depressing sameness in modern design. No actual anuses were consulted during this research, though several designers were clearly thinking about them.

如果您喜欢所看到的内容，您会在我的 Twitter 上找到更多类似的内容。
